dialog.stepLabel: Mysterious Man: "Hmm, let's give it another go... Low frequency to level 3......"

items.speaker: Mysterious Man
items.text: Hmm, let's give it another go... Low frequency to level 3... Getting minimal biological response. What if I set interference to half?

items.speaker: Mysterious Man
items.text: Waveband convergence rate at 67%, 70%... 75%... Steadily increasing. It's working!

options.text: Medium long hair, pale and skinny... You must be Jiuyue?
What's that... thing?
What are you doing?

items.speaker: Mysterious Man
items.text: Shhh, shush...

items.speaker: Mysterious Man
items.text: Be quiet. It's a crucial moment!

items.speaker: Mysterious Man
items.text: "You"... Hmm. "You and I"... Sing... "Song"?

items.speaker: Mysterious Man
items.text: ...Sky... What's that supposed to mean? And what is the last word? Love... Sorrow?

items.speaker: Mysterious Man
items.text: No, hold it, I need to hear it one more time... I didn't catch all of it...

items.speaker: Mysterious Man
items.text: ...Too late. The signal's already gone...

options.text: Am I allowed to talk now?
So, you're Jiuyue, right?

items.speaker: Mysterious Man
items.text: What? Oh, excuse me! I've been trying to understand this <color=Highlight>"phantom"</color>, and I got overexcited for a moment... And yes, please talk anytime you want!

items.speaker: Mysterious Man
items.text: I'm Jiuyue, a researcher studying the fog in Whining Aix's Mire, from the Academy. I've been researching TD Ecoacoustics in this area.

options.text: You can call me {PlayerName}.
{PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: {PlayerName}... I'll be sure to remember that name.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: I believe you had a question for me? Please, do ask away.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Umm, I am. Yet I don't believe you and I are acquainted?

options.text: You can call me {PlayerName}.
{PlayerName}. Pleasure to meet your acquaintance.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: {PlayerName}, I see... Ah, and what is it that you wish to ask of me?

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: As you may have realized, I'm in the middle of investigating the fog-related peculiarities of this area. If it's something unrelated, I'm afraid it'll have to wait.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: The Rangers back at the supply station wanted to make sure you were safe. I'm here to help you with the investigation.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Assist me? You're implying that you wish to accompany me in completing my research?

options.text: Oh, you don't want my help?
Relax, someone already paid me.
Do you need to act surprised?

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: No, feel free to come along! Academia may have its rules, but research has no limits. If you have the drive and determination to seek knowledge, you can become a researcher at any time!

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: I see. The Rangers at the station have already made a formal request for you to assist me in my research. This means that you have already reached an agreement on this matter.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: No, not at all. It's just that it's been a while since I have worked with anyone, so my reaction was a bit exaggerated.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: With some extra hands, we can accomplish more. I would definitely welcome your help. Besides, the people at the supply station can rest easy with you around.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: I didn't mean to sneak out alone and leave them behind. I just couldn't bear to wait another second before trying out my idea.

options.text: Your idea? Like... what you were doing just now?
That thing by your side just now, was it a "phantom"?

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Oh, that? That was merely... a small experiment in my research.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: I was indeed looking for a way to enter the fog. It was until I actually met a phantom.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: It was my first time seeing one in person. Definitely not the rumored Waveworn Phenomenon, but more like an information-filled body that could be decoded.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: So, what information does it carry, you may wonder? Could it be related to the cause of the fog?

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: With this possibility in mind, I followed the "phantom" all the way here. Which leads to how I was using the decoder on my Terminal to decipher it.

options.text: A decoder... Sounds very high-tech.
I think I got it, and then again I don't.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Well, certainly. I've improved upon it myself, based on the rudimentary version at the Academy.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: The new design can automatically recognize and analyze sounds, making it great for dealing with unknown sources like "phantoms."

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Um, you seem like you're even more confused than you were before? Ahhh, Hui told me not to overload people with principles and terms first thing when I meet them, and here I go again...

options.text: Phew... Good thing you stopped yourself in time.
No worries. I'll take it as a science class on the spot.
Who's Hui?

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Hehe, Hui is my childhood friend. She is the most brilliant researcher I've ever laid eyes on... and my beautiful beloved wife.

options.text: Now you're just plain showing off.
Well, isn't that true love if I ever saw one...

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Anyway... The Ecoacoustics theories are indeed quite complex, and it also involves information transcription and such. It's difficult to explain in just a few words.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Simply put, with this device, we can decipher what the "phantom" is saying.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Hmm, it's hard to explain how this machine works in a few words. Basically, it helps us understand what the "phantom" is saying and what they mean. Kinda cool, right?

options.text: But it doesn't seem to be working so well.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Uh, yeah. "You", "I", "Song", "Sky", "Love", "Sorrow"... these incoherent words comprise the entirety of its message, but the meaning they point to...

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: {PlayerName}, what do you make of these words?

options.text: They seem like a broken and encrypted poem.
I don't understand, but they sound beautiful somehow.
Doesn't sound like it has anything to do with the fog.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Hmm? A poem?

options.text: Songs rise above the sky, as love overcomes sorrow.
Songs of you and I will rid the sky of sorrow.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: I see... With your added interpretation, it does indeed appear more romantic. It's surprising to see that you have such a creative side.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Guess we can rule out the possible connection between the "phantoms" and the fog out there. After all, those anomalies are nowhere near as beautiful as poetry.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Indeed these words evoke a gentle and comforting imagery.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Guess we can rule out the possible connection between the "phantoms" and the fog out there. After all, those anomalies are nowhere near as beautiful as poetry.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Perhaps you are right. At least for now, I cannot see any connection between the "phantoms" and the fog through these words.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Well, at least that's what I deduced from my first encounter with it. I might be proven wrong later on, which is also natural. For now, let's focus on how to get to the center of the mist.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: I've been digging through all the data on Whining Aix's Mire, and I think I found a way to make it work. We just have to find the site, reboot the device, clear the fog, and investigate the source!

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Luckily, I had brought the ultrasonic pulse chips with me before setting off, so I can share them directly with you.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: The actions I just mentioned involve traversing through the marshlands, but don't worry, as long as we have these chips, we don't need to fear the tornado... Wait, where's my backpack?

options.text: What backpack?
I don't think you had a backpack when we met.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: I'm referring to the backpack in which I keep the equipment for investigation.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Oh no, how can I lose a large backpack like that? All those things in the bag... What about the chips?

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Hmm... It was when I met you—no, it was when I was chasing the... Yes, I was chasing after the "phantom"... I got so caught up, I probably forgot my bag somewhere!

options.text: Good thing you remember where it is. Let's go find it.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Alright, we should make haste and return to where I was before.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: *sigh* I got way over my head back there. It's good to have additional verification for my deductions, but it's not right to let it interfere with our original plan.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Now I can see why Hui never lets me go on investigations on my own...

dialog.stepLabel: Jiuyue: "Let's see... Perfect, nothing seems to be missing...."

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Let's see... Perfect, nothing seems to be missing.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Thank you, {PlayerName}. I'll be sure to keep a firm eye on it from now on!

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Now... About the <color=Highlight>ultrasonic pulse chips</color> we've discussed earlier. Here, put it inside your Terminal.

options.text: What does it do?

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Dear me. It appears you lack the necessary knowledge regarding the Mire. This calls for a science lesson!

options.text: Science lesson, yes. Complicated terms, no please.
I'm all ears, Professor Jiuyue.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: No need to worry, this will not involve any complex principles.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: This place is called the Whining Aix's Mire. Named after the bird-like Tacet Discord, <color=Highlight>Mourning Aix</color>, that lives here. Additionally, as you can see, this place has a lot of marshes.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Because of water temperature differences, there's a large <color=Highlight>vortex</color> deep in the marsh. It used to be considered a local spectacle, but now with the bizarre fog, it has stirred up dangerous <color=Highlight>mud</color> and spilled it everywhere.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: By installing these chips, we can utilize the resonance of ultrasonic pulses to dissolve the <color=Highlight>mud</color>.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: It's hard to explain, so let me show you. Watch that mud over there.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: I'll demonstrate what I mean.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Well? Rather effective, isn't it?

options.text: Easy as breeze
Well, at least it does the job.
I'll say it's got room for improvements.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: As I predicted. You seemed like the adaptable type.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Good. Should we encounter more mud later on, do give it a few more runs. Practice makes perfect.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Ah, yes. I'm still optimizing its functionality and handling. But this current version is what we have that works. But do share your detailed feedback with me once we've completed the investigation.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Now, with the chips installed, our prep work is nearly complete... Now back to the detailed plan of this operation. The first question —

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: How do we access the center of the fog for further investigations?

options.text: It's all yours, Professor Jiuyue.
Come on, enough with the suspense.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Ooh. Now you're making me nervous.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Alright, Alright.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: I'm not sure if the Rangers at the station told you or not. But all this fog, thunderstorms, and that huge vortex I just mentioned? They didn't just show up overnight.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: The exact spot where it began is now covered in thick fog, so we do know where these odd events originated.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: But we still don't know what caused them in this supposedly deepest area. The fog's thick, and wandering into it without caution could mean getting lost forever.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: I read that the <ano=CSC>Court of Savantae Committee</ano> once built a dedicated facility here. They even developed <color=Highlight>suppressors</color> to disperse the fog from the inside.

options.text: But the fog is still here.
You mentioned reactivating these suppressors earlier.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Yes. Despite of the well-intended attempts of the CSC scholars, the research facilities were eventually abandoned. Without proper maintenance, the suppressors were down soon after and couldn't be located.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: With these in mind, our priority is to locate the <color=Highlight>abandoned facility</color> in order to acquire the <color=Highlight>suppressors'</color> position and their activation key.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: We'll reactivate the suppressors and dissipate the fog as we progress. We should uncover everything once we reach the deepest place.

options.text: Sure is a plan filled with twists and turns.
Is there no easier way?

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: I agree. Should any aspect of the plan not be executed properly, the entire plan may come to a halt.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: However, isn't it part of humanity to not back down when faced with the unknown?  If there is a way, we shall try it out and eliminate the uncertainty along the way.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Hmm... that's all there is to it. If it could be easily resolved, it wouldn't have been a lingering problem that has troubled everyone for so long.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: But on the other hand, when you think about it, if we can solve even such a challenging problem, aren't we incredibly capable?

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: I have confidence, as I won't be alone this time, you will be accompanying me.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: This will be my first time working together with anyone other than Hui. As my wife, she has always taken care of me, and as a researcher, she is among the most passionate and outstanding individuals.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Though she's not with me here today, I trust I can successfully complete the investigation with you, my new partner!

options.text: Now I'm feeling pumped!
Fine, can't argue with your optimism.
So why isn't Hui with you this time?

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Hahaha. Yes! This is the spirit of science!

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: I'll take that as a compliment.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Well... This isn't the first time. We've been apart for a while now. Sometimes, it just can't be helped, you know?

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Anyway, when the situation is against us, all we are left to do... is to put ourselves to work!

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Now, without further ado, let us set out to the settlement closest to the fog.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: During my previous investigation, residents of that settlement mentioned someone named Qisheng who ran into an unknown CSC building while scavenging. It's likely the facility I've read about in the logs.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: I've arranged a meeting with him. We should inquire him about the facility's location.

dialog.stepLabel: Jiuyue: "It just appeared behind you out of nowhere... I know it does..."

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: It just appeared behind you out of nowhere... I know it doesn't hurt anyone, but still...

options.text: I thought you already saw it once.
Looks exactly like how they described at the supply station.
My heart missed a beat for a moment there...

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Well, last time I was the one chasing after it, not it jump scaring me!

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Huh? So you've heard about it from the station. Seems like the number of eyewitnesses are beginning to grow.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Fear not. I-if anything does happen, I'll make sure to protect you!

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Besides... it only scared us because we weren't expecting it. Now, it's just hovering over there like... a harmless puppet?

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: But it appeared right by our side out of all the places it could be... It's almost as if it were actively seeking any person passing by.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Like the phantom I was following. It immediately stopped moving after realizing I was behind it.

options.text: Sounds like it was waiting for you...
Strange. I wonder what kind of message it's carrying?
Quick! Use your decoder!

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Indeed. Essentially, the information entities serve as a medium for carrying information, and they are not living beings. But perhaps their impulse to convey information can be considered a kind of instinct.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: They yearn to be understood, like how we yearn to understand them.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Now that it has come to us, naturally, it's time to bring out... my frequency transcription program!

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Ha, beat you there! I have already initiated the frequency transcription program.

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: (Jiuyue points his Terminal towards the "phantom", committing full concentration as he reads out the results.)

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Death... I, cannot return... Resurrect... Not...

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Forget me... F-farewell?

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: L-leave... No, wait. There's still more... And it's gone.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Death. I. Cannot return. Resurrect. Not. Forget me. Farewell... The words are making more sense this time. But it's still pretty hard to understand.

options.text: It's a different set of phrases.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Could it be that different "phantoms" carry different messages? No, that'll introduce too much noise and bring down the effectiveness of communication...

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: It would make more sense if one message was broken up into separate parts, and delivered piece-by-piece.

options.text: Some parts go here, some parts go there?
Like different segments of a poem.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Very much so. Yes.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Ahh a rather fitting and... sentimental analogy.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: It is possible that the phantoms each carry different bits of the full message. We need to collect all of them to truly decipher it.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: As for why and how the message is broken down: It could be purely random, or it could be constrained by the different sizes and energy capacities of the "phantoms".

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: After all, the information can get lost as they run out of energy. Like our two encounters with the "phantoms", we can only extract so much information from a single one.

options.text: Once their energy is exhausted, they disappear.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Precisely. But did you notice that this phantom we encountered inside the fog seems to be able to transmit more information?

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: It may be just a coincidence, but I presume the less distance it travels, the less energy it consumes.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Plus, so far they've only appeared within proximity of the fog, suggesting that... the fog may be their origin?

options.text: We should give it a further look once the fog's cleared.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Yes. As Hui mentioned before, Ecoacoustics and Ecology are two deeply connected disciplines of study. Through unraveling hidden secrets in the fog, we may gain a comprehensive understanding of these "phantoms."

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Let's press on. It's almost time for our meeting.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Owing to your assistance, perhaps, we were barely stalled or disrupted and made much more progress in decoding on this trip.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Having a partner that you can get along with really makes for an untroubled investigation.

dialog.stepLabel: Young Man: "You must be the researcher from the Academy?..."

items.speaker: Young Man
items.text: You must be the researcher from the Academy?

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Yes. I am Jiuyue of the Huaxu Academy, and this is {PlayerName}. We're here to seek information regarding the derelict Court of Savantae facility recently discovered.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Yet... If my memory serves me correctly, it was Qisheng who had made the discovery. And you are...?

items.speaker: Young Man
items.text: Hello. You can call me Qijie. Qisheng is my father. He was on his way to meet you but ran into a few wounded residents.

items.speaker: Qijie
items.text: Being a former army doctor, he wanted to take care of the injured before coming to see you.

options.text: So, are we supposed to...
Anything we can help you with?

items.speaker: Qijie
items.text: You'll have to wait around for a while. My father was the one who stumbled upon the building in a detour while he was out scavenging. You'll have to ask himself for the details.

items.speaker: Qijie
items.text: It'll be another hour at most before he's back. I'll let you know as soon as he gives me the word.

items.speaker: Qijie
items.text: For the sake of ease, I encourage you not to wander too far during this time. You may walk around the settlement if you'd like.

items.speaker: Qijie
items.text: This is the closest human settlement to the fog and to where the two avian TDs had nested years ago, which formed the unique landscape you see.

items.speaker: Qijie
items.text: Thank you. It's nothing my father can't handle on his own. The problem is he needs to get more medicine ASAP to handle the sudden influx of patients.

items.speaker: Qijie
items.text: Also... not only did we break our arrangement, it simply wouldn't be fair to just ask for your help.

items.speaker: Qijie
items.text: It'll be another hour at most before my father is done. I'll let you know as soon as he gives me the word.

items.speaker: Qijie
items.text: Do feel free to walk around the settlement if you're feeling bored.

items.speaker: Qijie
items.text: This is the closest human settlement to the fog and to where the two avian TDs had nested years ago, which formed the unique landscape you see.

options.text: Avian TDs... You mean the Mourning Aix?

items.speaker: Qijie
items.text: Yes, the Mourning Aix... Its cries are filled with mourn and shrill, randomly attacking any human that passes by. Like any other anomaly, it has caused us a great deal of trouble.

items.speaker: Qijie
items.text: But years ago, it didn't behave like this at all. When I was five or six years old, I first saw it with my father.

items.speaker: Qijie
items.text: It soared in the skies with another avian TD, singing to one another. Even as we stood close by, those TDs didn't pay us much attention, as if they only saw each other.

items.speaker: Qijie
items.text: Later I heard that the other TD had died, leaving the Mourning Aix behind. Since then, it has changed, and this whole place has changed with it.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Must be in a lot of pain to be left alone.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: And with so much pain, I presume it had to unleash it somehow...

options.text: It's getting sad all of a sudden...
Sounds like you know a lot of the Mourning Aix.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Just as the name would suggest.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Oh, do I...? Well, of course! I'm a dedicated researcher in solving Mire's anomalies, of course!

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Having this basic level of understanding of the issue is a basic requirement.

items.speaker: Qijie
items.text: It sounds like you're quite interested in the creature. If you want to know more, you can ask the elders in the settlement.

items.speaker: Qijie
items.text: For me, memories about the avian TD have become vague and hard to recall.

dialog.stepLabel: Qijie: "Thank you for your patience, Jiuyue, {PlayerName}. I hope yo..."

items.speaker: Qijie
items.text: Thank you for your patience, Jiuyue, {PlayerName}. I hope you've enjoyed your stay here so far.

items.speaker: Qijie
items.text: Father told me the wounded have been treated, and he is on his way here. Shall we head to our meeting place from earlier?

options.text: Sure. See you there!

items.speaker: Qisheng
items.text: Sorry for keeping you waiting.

options.text: Qijie told me all about it. Let's go help the wounded.
It's fine. We took the opportunity to learn about the situation.

items.speaker: Qisheng
items.text: As this fellow researcher had told me, you're here to ask about the Court of Savantae facility, correct?

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: According to the archives, the Court of Savantae once built a dedicated facility to study the origin of this fog. I guess it could be the one you've discovered.

items.speaker: Qisheng
items.text: A research facility for the fog... Hmm... That explains why the facility is equipped for observation, with environmental simulators and a central control panel in the center.

items.speaker: Qisheng
items.text: I tried using it. Was still in working condition, but it needed authorization credentials or something.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: No need to worry! The Huaxu Academy has dedicated procedures set in place when it comes to matters relating to the Court of Savantae. Prior to my departure, Mr. Xiangli Yao told me everything I needed to know.

items.speaker: Qisheng
items.text: I will share with you the coordinates of the facility. But... are you really planning on going there?

options.text: What's the problem?

items.speaker: Qisheng
items.text: I'm aware that you must've done a great deal of research regarding the fog. Yet once you've gone inside, you'll soon realize that the reality is completely different.

items.speaker: Qisheng
items.text: *sigh* With all the TDs growing ever so restless, plus those weird "phantoms"... and the mud, it's all over the place...

options.text: Don't worry, it's nothing we can't handle.
We've got everything in control.
Jiuyue already told me all there is to it.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Yes. With {PlayerName} and I's unparalleled teamwork, there isn't a task we can't accomplish.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: That's right, leave all the science to me. We won't set up to fail.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Hehe. I was just doing my job... After all, you are my partner in this investigation.

items.speaker: Qisheng
items.text: I understand that you have your reasons for your persistence. But still, do prioritize your safety above all else.

items.speaker: Qisheng
items.text: Let me think... Here, I'll mark all the routes and locations I used to reach the facility on the map for you. Follow them closely, and you'll be safe.

items.speaker: Qisheng
items.text: Take all the supplies you need from the settlement before you go.

dialog.stepLabel: Jiuyue: "This must be the control panel Qisheng spoke of...."

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: This must be the control panel Qisheng spoke of.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: By the looks of it, the facility has been abandoned for quite some time, and no one's tampered with these controls since.

options.text: Why was it abandoned?
What... happened to this place?

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: I... do not know. I failed to find any relevant information when I was studying the archives.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: But sometimes, research gets cut short because of funding, outside pressure, or other issues. It's not always the researchers' choice.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Our predecessors were unable to find an answer, but at least they left behind these clues. It's on us to keep their legacy alive and move forward.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Hmm... It's is still in working condition, and there's enough backup power... Now, according to Mr. Xiangli Yao's instructions... Username, and then...

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: There, I'm in!

dialog.stepLabel: Jiuyue: "These are videos related to the avian TDs...."

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: These are videos related to the avian TDs.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: In the first footage, the two can be seen flying together, circling each other as if they are in a chorus... Similar to that of what Qijié and the settlers had described. It must be when the other one was still alive.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: It matches what researcher wrote in his log!

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Could it be that they were the ones that built this facility? We should watch the other video...

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Second video. The Mourning Aix is flying through the fog. It seems to be carrying something in its talons, and thunderstorms occuring behind it...

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Does this mean the fog was caused by the Aix? Has it been providing the fog with energy all this time?

items.speaker: "Phantom"
items.text: Sizzle

items.speaker: "Phantom"
items.text: “Bzzz Bzzz... Bzzz... Bzzz...”

options.text: Quick! Pull out your decoder!
Here we go again. More “phantoms”...
It’s like they were waiting for you to get an answer.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: “You and I together... Our songs... Bright skies... Love...”

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: “Death. I... cannot return... Resurrect... Not... Forget me. Farewell.”

options.text: They’re the same messages from before, but more complete.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Two pieces of information, released by a single “phantom”... The “phantoms" inside the fog do indeed carry more information, and also grants better decoding results.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Now. Do you feel that the scenes in which these two pieces of messages correspond... feel a bit familiar?

options.text: It’s the Mourning Aix and the other avian TD.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: They once sang together across the clear sky and also experienced separation through life and death. The Mourning Aix never once forgot its partner.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: By not accepting what it has lost, it can never bear to let go and move on... The tone of these messages, feels more like a story told by the one who had experienced it...

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: “You” are unable to return, so “you” hope it will forget “you”. “You” mentioned saying goodbye, thus...

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Yet. Who are “you”?

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: If the “phantoms" are just couriers of information, then who are “you” exactly?

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: The air suddenly grew silent. As you listen to Jiuyue quietly mumble his questions, you cast your eyes on the "phantom.” You feel that the answer is within graps and all that’s missing is an opportunity .

items.speaker: "Phantom"
items.text: “Bzzz... Bzzz Bzzz... Fzzz Fzzz... Bzzz Bzzz... Bzzz... Bzzz, Fzzz Fzzz.”

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: “No reborn... Go on, pointless... You will get hurt...”

items.speaker: "Phantom"
items.text: “Bzzz... Bzzz Bzzz... Fzzz Fzzz... Bzzz Bzzz... Bzzz... Bzzz, Fzzz Fzzz.”

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: “Do not hurt. Yourself... Stop. I. Leave...”

items.speaker: "Phantom"
items.text: “Fzzz... Bzzz Bzzz... Fzzz Fzzz...”

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: “Say. Goodbye...”

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: “Lest us say goodbye...”

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: As Jiuyue continues to decode its messages, the sounds of the “phantom” began to crackle, ultimately fading away like the others before.

options.text: This “phantom” used up all its energy.
Hey. Is something wrong?

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Huh? Oh, nothing. I-it’s just that I...

options.text: Cheer up. We got a lot more out of it than the last ones.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Yes, I'm okay. I just had some thoughts about the "I" and "you" from the messages that’s all.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Although it may sound bizarre, but I think... that this should be the most reasonable possibility.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Recall that researcher “12” mentioned in his log that the Mourning Aix's vital signs grew weak each time it left the fog.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: It never ceased providing energy for the source, even at the cost of its own well-being. It must think it’s a necessary sacrifice.

options.text: All for the sake of its partner?
The messages mentioned "reborn"...

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: The answer is rather clear now.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: The source with strong energy fluctuations and an absence of vital signs that we detected in the depths of the fog is a likely indication the Mourning Aix is trying to resurrect the other avian TD.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: We may find remains of TDs gathered around it.

options.text: Wait a second. Resurrect? A TD? Is that even possible?

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Resurrection... it’s out of the question beyond any doubt.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: The river you step into again will never be the same river you stepped into for the first time, and things that perish cannot truly be resurrected.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: All the Mourning Aix’s doing is merely self-harm... A perseverance to a impossible outcome. Not only that, by doing this it has generated energy anomalies across the area like the fog the mud.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: As the fallen avian TD cannot be resurrected, the accumulation of TD remains carry Reverberation frequencies, think of it as an of scraps of consciousness...

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Acting out of instinct, it transmits information in the form of "phantoms.” Only the “phantoms” ran into random people wandering on the ground, instead of reaching the Aix flying in the sky.

options.text: The “I” must be the Mourning Aix.
Sounds like the “phantoms” got the addressee of the love letter wrong.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Yes. If that's the case, then everything now makes sense.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Yes. A love letter that has spanned across life and death.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: The fallen TD repeated its calls over and over again, hoping that the Mourning Aix would halt its efforts and bid farewell, the latter is completely unaware of it. Can it really let go of its obsession as the other hoped?

options.text: Should be possible. They have deep feelings for each other, after all.
It's hard to say. Their feelings go way too deep.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Emotion can be the most uncontrollable force... It can drive an individual to do anything, an can also be there to support them to stand up again and start anew.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: We haven't activated all the suppressors yet, and not even reached the source of the fog, but we've already sorted out the cause of the fog.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: According to the description of our tasks and mission procedures, if the source of the fog is the accumulation of the TD remains, activating the final suppressor is all there’s left to do.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: According to procedure, we should report the Mourning Aix’s situation to the Midnight Rangers and have the professionals handle the remains.

options.text: Sounds to me you have other plans.
Wouldn’t want to miss out after all we’ve been through.
You’ve said before that speculations need to be verified.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Yes. For the settlers of the Whining Aix's Mire, a thorough extermination of the remains may be the most direct and efficient way, but may not be the best.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Carefully gathered remains, only to have them cleared and its final hope shattered by the humans. This might arouse the Mourning Aix's hatred towards the humans. It may go berserk.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: And... if the Aix fails to accept the loss of its partner and give up resurrection, maybe it will try even harder, again and again, endlessly triggering the anomaly.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Yes, it's as if being only one step away from the finish line. We have to do something to perfectly wrap up this investigation for the both of us.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Perhaps... only by truly accepting the loss and giving up the idea of resurrection can we prevent the Mourning Aix from trying again and causing another anomaly.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Carefully gathered remains, only to have them cleared and its final hope shattered by the humans. This might arouse the Mourning Aix's hatred towards the humans. It may go berserk.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: And... if the Aix fails to accept the loss of its partner and give up resurrection, maybe it will try even harder, again and again, endlessly triggering the anomaly.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: You're right, all of these are based on my personal understanding... of hypothetical inferences.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Even if it is infinitely close to reality, with a probability of up to 99.999%, this cannot be used ad the true answer without verification.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Yes. For the settlers of the Whining Aix's Mire, a thorough extermination of the remains may be the most direct and efficient way, but may not be the best.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Carefully gathered remains, only to have them cleared and its final hope shattered by the humans. This might arouse the Mourning Aix's hatred towards the humans. It may go berserk.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Perhaps... only by truly accepting the loss and giving up the idea of resurrection can we prevent the Mourning Aix from trying again and causing another anomaly.

options.text: You wish to help the Mourning Aix let go of its persistence.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Have it let go of its persistence... It might sound a bit too optimistic and sad.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: It reminded me of what was written inside the researcher's log, of the Mourning Aix's plea to humans as its companion died, and its never-ending cries of sorrow.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: I honestly believe that it is capable of feeling the same emotions as humans.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: With the passing of the one it holds dear, it cannot forget, and does not wish to forget. So it shackled itself in place, entrusting everything to nothingness...

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: If we can find a way to help them meet face-to-face for one last time, have its companion convey its feelings to the Aix directly—

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: “Stop, let it go, pull yourself together. Start over, and move on.”

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: This is something... only the other half can do.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: We need to adjust our plans. Once we’ve activated the remaining suppressor, we head straight to the source, but not just to figure out the cause.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: But to attempt to solve the cause from its roots.

options.text: Right on. It’s just an extra step.
You won't know unless you try.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: <color=Highlight>Jiuyue:</color> The debugging is complete, but we need your power to sustain the projection in the beginning—Wait! It's gone rogue!

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: <color=Highlight>Jiuyue:</color> Where is it going... the remnants of Reverberation can't hold out too long... if it keeps up like this... Are we going to miss out on the last chance, too?

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: <color=Highlight>Jiuyue:</color> Rover, please! Take the bird-shaped TD with you, catch up with it, and calm it down!

dialog.stepLabel: Calm Midnight Ranger: "Thank you for your help back there. Could have been a tough ..."

items.speaker: Calm Midnight Ranger
items.text: Thank you for your help back there. Could have been a tough fight.

items.speaker: Calm Midnight Ranger
items.text: Their leader was right, though. Most of our manpower's occupied with all the mess that's been going on.

options.text: They sounded well aware of your supply situation
Didn't seem like an even fight, so I had to help out.

items.speaker: Calm Midnight Ranger
items.text: They've likely been scouting our supply station for a while. Choose to strike right at our weakest.

items.speaker: Calm Midnight Ranger
items.text: Weather here in the Whining Aix's Mire isn't the kindest. The area is covered with fog most of the year, plus the frequent thunderstorms.

items.speaker: Calm Midnight Ranger
items.text: These bandits are more than willing to risk their lives just to get their hands on some extra supplies. Attacking civilian settlements, raiding convoys... We've had our share of engagements with them.

items.speaker: Calm Midnight Ranger
items.text: Didn't expect that they would come straight for our supply station this time.

items.speaker: Young Midnight Ranger
items.text: Hmph. Bastards must've gotten desperate for coming after our supplies.

items.speaker: Young Midnight Ranger
items.text: I heard from a few civilians at the nearby settlement that supplies are getting harder to come in these days. The TDs' gotten more ruthless, get too close and they'd chase you down no matter what.

items.speaker: Young Midnight Ranger
items.text: And on top of all that... some folks' been saying they're seeing phantoms lately...

options.text: Why did it turn into a horror story all of a sudden?
Hey, no one said anything about getting spooked...
For real? And you saw them yourself?

items.speaker: Young Midnight Ranger
items.text: *sigh* You need to lay off on the late-night podcasts...

items.speaker: Young Midnight Ranger
items.text: And I'm not making this up. People saw them with their own eyes, I swear!

items.speaker: Young Midnight Ranger
items.text: No, no. I wasn't trying to scare you. I just heard from people who actually saw phantoms. I swear on my next paycheck!

items.speaker: Young Midnight Ranger
items.text: Umm. I didn't see any myself... But those who actually did told me about it, so it must be true, right?

items.speaker: Calm Midnight Ranger
items.text: Anyway. Just consider this as a reminder. The area inside behind us is as dangerous as it looks on the outside.

items.speaker: Calm Midnight Ranger
items.text: But before you decide to venture further, you should go talk to Jiuyue. He's a researcher the Academy sent here to investigate all the recent happenings. He'd know more about the stuff than we do.

items.speaker: Young Midnight Ranger
items.text: Err. About Jiuyue... You may be out of luck trying to find him at the moment.

items.speaker: Calm Midnight Ranger
items.text: What do you mean... Wait a minute, don't tell me he went into the fog alone again? How many times have I told him not to go off wandering alone? Ugh...

items.speaker: Calm Midnight Ranger
items.text: And you. Why didn't you tell me about this earlier?

items.speaker: Young Midnight Ranger
items.text: Well, I was on my way of telling you... if we didn't run into those Exiles...

items.speaker: Calm Midnight Ranger
items.text: The supply station haven't got any extra men to spare, and the Exiles might try to make another run at us. But I can't just leave a civilian like Jiuyue on his own out there.

items.speaker: Calm Midnight Ranger
items.text: {Male=Mr.;Female=Miss} Resonator, I... There's something I want to discuss with you.

options.text: Want me to help you out?
You stay here. I'll go help Jiuyue.

items.speaker: Calm Midnight Ranger
items.text: By all means. Please. Under current circumstances, I can't think of a better way.

items.speaker: Calm Midnight Ranger
items.text: If it isn't too much of a bother, find Jiuyue, and accompany him in his investigation.

items.speaker: Young Midnight Ranger
items.text: Do you really mean it? That's fantastic!

items.speaker: Young Midnight Ranger
items.text: Ha! I just knew it you had a kind heart underneath those skills of yours!

items.speaker: Young Midnight Ranger
items.text: Hehe, I knew it! I knew I saw a kind and generous heart beneath those skills of yours...

options.text: Ah, yes. Praise me more!
Now now, easy with the flattery...
Hold on, I see where this is going...

items.speaker: Young Midnight Ranger
items.text: Ahem, well...

options.text: Oh boy. Here it comes...

items.speaker: Calm Midnight Ranger
items.text: What is it? Are you holding out on me? Spit it out!

items.speaker: Young Midnight Ranger
items.text: Well, I guess it was a little too much. But those were honest show of gratitude and compliments!

items.speaker: Young Midnight Ranger
items.text: It's just that... I do have a small extra favor to ask. Hehe.

items.speaker: Calm Midnight Ranger
items.text: What is it? What am I missing?

items.speaker: Calm Midnight Ranger
items.text: Yeah, you sound suspicious, boy. What are you holding out on me? Spit it out!

items.speaker: Young Midnight Ranger
items.text: No, no. It's Jiuyue. You know, him being an Academy researcher and all, everytime he discovers something new, he'd be running off into the fog for more research.

items.speaker: Young Midnight Ranger
items.text: Navigating through the Mire is already hard enough. Plus the "phantom" incidents settlement residents have been talking about. It's a spooky fest out there.

items.speaker: Young Midnight Ranger
items.text: A non-Resonator like Jiuyue off on his own is one thing. With the outpost short on manpower, there's no guarantee that those Exiles won't come at us again...

items.speaker: Young Midnight Ranger
items.text: So I was thinking if you could lend us a hand and assist Jiuyue in his research.

options.text: Umm... We're busting ghosts now?
This just got to a whole new level.

items.speaker: Young Midnight Ranger
items.text: No, not at all. It's just a figure of speech! Witnesses described it as a blurry, shapeless, dark cloud, like a ghost...

items.speaker: Young Midnight Ranger
items.text: I wouldn't dare have you go risking yourself.

items.speaker: Young Midnight Ranger
items.text: Don't you worry, us Rangers never let goodwill go unpaid!

items.speaker: Calm Midnight Ranger
items.text: Sure, I'll put in a good word to get you a fat paycheck for helping out with the government research!

options.text: Now you're speaking my language.
Pay or no pay, I'm just here to help out.
Great. As long as I can help out.

items.speaker: Young Midnight Ranger
items.text: Okay. I'll send you the coordinates of the general area Jiuyue is likely to be in. He should be somewhere near the marshlands. He's got medium-long hair, with a pale complexion, skinny. You'll know it's him once you see him.

items.speaker: Calm Midnight Ranger
items.text: Although you're there to assist him in his research, but don't hesitate to stop him if he's trying anything irrational.

items.speaker: Calm Midnight Ranger
items.text: He's obsessed with TD Ecoacoustics and can go overexcited sometimes. Almost made himself a mudman diving into a swamp for data last time. Can't handle another episode like that, no way!

dialog.stepLabel: Jiuyue: "Are you saying the Mourning Aix may have caused this fog?..."

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Are you saying the Mourning Aix may have caused this fog?

items.speaker: Jinfang
items.text: What the...? Who even are you?

options.text: Jiuyue... You came on too strong.
We just happened to overhear your conversation...

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Please pardon the interruption. I'm a researcher from the Huaxu Academy on a mission to investigate the fog. Please, I need to learn more about what you've just said.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Yes, I'm a researcher from the Huaxu Academy on a mission to investigate the fog. Please, I need to learn more about what you've just said.

items.speaker: Huayuan
items.text: Well... I guess there's no harm in telling you. It's not like I'm making it up.

items.speaker: Huayuan
items.text: We've seen it many times now. The Mourning Aix enters the fog and stays there for a while. But once it gets out from there, a big lightning strikes, and the fog thickens.

items.speaker: Huayuan
items.text: The Aix must have done something!

items.speaker: Jinfang
items.text: You said you're looking into all this mess, so... you got any conclusions yet? When are we getting rid of this fog?

items.speaker: Jinfang
items.text: You're our best bet now. Please make it go away already!

options.text: We are on this case.
We'll figure it out.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Rest assured, we'll get to the bottom of it!

dialog.stepLabel: Xiaomu: "I-I just can't do it, Grandpa. I can't even get a melody goi..."

items.speaker: Xiaomu
items.text: I-I just can't do it, Grandpa. I can't even get a melody going, and my cheeks are getting sore...

items.speaker: Uncle Bai
items.text: That's because you're doing it wrong, kiddo!

items.speaker: Xiaomu
items.text: Can we take a break for just a little... Huh? Are you here for my grandpa?

options.text: Yes. But we can wait...

items.speaker: Xiaomu
items.text: Well, I can't have you waiting on me. Ask away.

items.speaker: Xiaomu
items.text: Hehe. Then that means I can take a breath for a while!

items.speaker: Uncle Bai
items.text: You little rascal... Ahem. What can this old man do for you, strangers?

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Oh, it's nothing serious. We noticed you were teaching your grandson to play music on a leaf. Can it really make the Mourning Aix sing along with you?

items.speaker: Uncle Bai
items.text: Sure it can. It's something that was uniquely popular among us older generations. And all you need, get ready for it...

items.speaker: Uncle Bai
items.text: Is just an ordinary leaf.

options.text: A leaf? You sure you can really play music with it.
Wow, that's indeed a big surprise.

items.speaker: Xiaomu
items.text: See, Grandpa? They don't believe you either!

items.speaker: Uncle Bai
items.text: Shush! Go away, you brat.

items.speaker: Uncle Bai
items.text: Find a leaf and pinch it together on one side like this... hold the other side close to your lips. Now blow along the edge to make sounds. You may even change the pitch—

options.text: Sounds a lot like a bird's singing.

items.speaker: Uncle Bai
items.text: Indeed. You can turn anything in the world into an instrument as long as you've got the music in you. If you put your heart into it, you can even use it to express your feelings.

items.speaker: Uncle Bai
items.text: When we were young, we would sit on the cliff after a long day of work and play some simple tunes with the leaves.

items.speaker: Uncle Bai
items.text: The Mourning Aix and the other bird TD rarely attacked anyone, and we'd watch the two always flying right beside each other off in the distance, back and forth like a lovely couple.

items.speaker: Uncle Bai
items.text: Some random fellow then suggested that we use the leaves to say hello. So we gave it a try, and to our surprise, not only did they understand us, they even eventually joined in singing to our tunes.

items.speaker: Uncle Bai
items.text: Boy, those were the chill and relaxing days.

options.text: So sad that the good times didn't last...
So now only one of the two TDs is left?

items.speaker: Uncle Bai
items.text: Yep. Good times never last.

items.speaker: Uncle Bai
items.text: Yep. All because of what happened afterwards...

items.speaker: Uncle Bai
items.text: There was a TD Outbreak... Every TD out there was hungry for the sounds. Human or not human, they'd tear everything to shreds.

items.speaker: Uncle Bai
items.text: Trying to protect its partner, one of the "birds" were attacked and chewed up alive by rampant TDs. The one that survived went mad later because of it, becoming the Mourning Aix we all know today.

items.speaker: Uncle Bai
items.text: Perhaps from its perspective, time forever froze at that moment. From then on, it lived like a zombie, denying and attacking all living things that approached it.

items.speaker: Xiaomu
items.text: So... you're saying the reason why big "birdie" became the Mourning Aix, is because it lost its loved one?

items.speaker: Xiaomu
items.text: Like Mrs. Qiu. After Mr. Qiu passed away, she no longer made delicious rice cakes like she used to. Now they taste either salty or bitter.

items.speaker: Uncle Bai
items.text: A bit too early to be getting into these things for your age, don't you think, my boy?

options.text: You're quite smart.
Sometimes kids can see things grown-ups don't.

items.speaker: Xiaomu
items.text: Hehe. I learned it all from Grandpa!

items.speaker: Uncle Bai
items.text: Why you little... You're in for it now. You'll be practicing extra hours as punishment!

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: In time, one must accept their losses before truly moving on. It doesn't do to dwell on it.

options.text: But it's hard to move on from such losses.
Agreed. Very important to stay positive.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Yeah, it's tough. But there are things I'd always feel driven to do, no matter what.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Things like... exploring the unknown! Like this investigation!

options.text: Amazing how you always find ways to talk about your research.
I knew you'd say that again.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Now, let's push on!

dialog.stepLabel: Jiuyue: "Ahh... And there they go, flying off into the distance. Unde..."

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Ahh... And there they go, flying off into the distance. Understandable, as they should make the most out of their final goodbye before their energy is completely exhausted.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: An unthinkable reunion they’ve been yearning for all this time... A reunion that not everyone has the luxury to experience.

options.text: Are you talking about the Mourning Aix or...?
The person you wish to reunite... Hui?

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Hmm... Seems like you caught on to it.

options.text: It was obvious to tell from your words.
It’s an understanding only someone who’s experienced it can grasp.
It's just an educated guess.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: You’re right. Love is something that simply cannot be hidden from sight, especially since... Hui’s my most beloved sugar-plum in the whole universe.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: I tried my best to maintain an objective view, but it seems I just couldn't help myself.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Aha! It was my reaction that’s led to the verification of your speculations. Very cunning, {PlayerName}!

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Alas, I’m sure you’d know... Love is something that simply cannot be hidden from sight, especially since... Hui’s my most beloved sugar-plum in the whole universe.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Although Hui has parted this world for ten months and thirteen days... I still miss her so much.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: She was the greatest researcher of them all. She’d always venture deep into Tacet Fields to acquire the latest data on the TDs.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: If only I had been at her side that one time, death wouldn’t have separated us at least. In the end, I wasn’t there for her, so she never came back, and I... am remained here.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: To certain extent, what happened afterwards was comparable to that of the Mourning Aix.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: I refused to accept her departure, and lived as though entirely stripped of life... I also held on to the illusion that as a researcher, I may be able to find a way to bring Hui back to my side.

options.text: She wouldn’t want you to if she’s here.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Yes. As Xiangli Yao had said to me as well.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: How can anyone bear to see the one you deeply love live in a daze? You’d wish them the best and move on.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Even though left alone, there’s no need to be depressed. All there is for me to do is to carry the memories we shared, our past, and continue to move forward and live a good life. This is what Hui would hope for me to do.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: I’m sure its what the avian TD would tell the Mourning Aix in their goodbye.

options.text: I wonder how their farewell’s going.
Do you believe the Mourning Aix is capable of completely letting it go?

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: It's hard to say, but I’m hopeful that it’ll be happy ending.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: If its capable of garning the determination to ressurect the other at the cost of inflicting self-harm. Then... I’m sure it’ll find a way to move on following it’s companion’s wishes.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Naturally, the Aix will have to endure a period of pain before achieving it, and saying goodbye is the first step to a fresh new beginning.

options.text: And for you as well.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: By all means.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Oh, by the way, have a look at this... I found it after the fog had cleared. Judging by its form, it's the same as the maintenance logs of the first two suppressors. Instead of a research record, this one appears to be more like a draft of a message.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: It's yet another emotionally biased writing. However... considering the things we encountered during the investigation, it seems rather fitting.

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: The “phantom” hovers in front of you. The sounds it makes seems to be clearer than before.

options.text: Do you... have something to say to us?
Is it... waiting for us to react to it?

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: “Follow me.”

options.text: Where to?

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: I do not know. It’s what the decoder is telling me what the “phantom” is implying at the moment.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Where is it trying to lead us?

dialog.stepLabel: Jiuyue: "I’d have to say, the wording in this log is rather emotional..."

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: I’d have to say, the wording in this log is rather emotionally biased, and even the conclusions are as equally far-fetched.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: However... considering how the the previous manual was labeled "Classified", it seems to me that SR-461 a body of a handful of smaller teams, so it’s understandable to have researchers of varying personalities.

options.text: What do you make of the speculations mentioned in the log?

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Which part are you referring to specifically? The likelihood that the source possesses human intelligence? Or the part regarding the avian TDs carrying human emotions?

options.text: The source possessing human intelligence.
the avian TD with human emotions.
Both.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Till this day, the Academy has yet to discover any signature of life signs from within the fog, yet I cannot use this as evidence to deny the researcher's speculations.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: While hypothesis is one matter, validity is another.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Nonetheless, I'm inclined to believe his theory holds merit. As we continue to prove the feasibility of the seemingly impossible, fewer are the regrets we have to deal with.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: That is the...

options.text: The spirit of science!

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Hahaha. Yes! The spirit of science compels me!

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Personally, I agree with his theory.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Nearly all TDs carry the instinct to hunt and devour "sound". They attack other living beings, even their own kind.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Yet the two avian TDs are uniquely different. As Qijié had described, they are mutually inseparable, they do not attack humans, only caring for themselves.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Perhaps they exist to complete the other.

options.text: The way you’re putting it makes it feel like they’re not even TDs.
Maybe you’ve added a touch of your own interpretation into it.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: There is yet to be a comprehensive definition of a “Tacet Discord,” is there not? There may even be countless more entities in the world that are beyond our imagination than meets the eye.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Haha! Plus, I’m sure you’d agree with me that it’s something which can't be helped! No human can trully relinquish the influences of experience and emotions, not even researchers!

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Speaking of the source... Till this day, the Academy has yet to discover any signature of life signs from within the fog, yet I cannot use this as evidence to deny the researcher's speculations.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: As mentioned in the log, a definitive judgment cannot be drawn without making direct contact with it. While hypothesis is one matter, validity is another.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Nonetheless, I'm inclined to believe his theory holds merit. As we continue to prove the feasibility of the seemingly impossible, fewer are the regrets we have to deal with.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: That is the...

options.text: The spirit of science!

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Hahaha. Yes! The spirit of science compels me!

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Moving on to his speculations regarding the avian TDs carrying human emotions. Personally, I share the same sentiment.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Nearly all TDs carry the instinct to hunt and devour "sound". They attack other living beings, even their own kind.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Yet the two avian TDs are uniquely different. As Qijié had described, they are mutually inseparable, they do not attack humans, only caring for themselves.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Perhaps they exist to complete the other.

options.text: The way you’re putting it makes it feel like they’re not even TDs.
Maybe you’ve added a touch of your own interpretation into it.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: There is yet to be a comprehensive definition of a “Tacet Discord,” is there not? There may even be countless more entities in the world that are beyond our imagination than meets the eye.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Haha! Plus, I’m sure you’d agree with me that it’s something which can't be helped! No human can trully relinquish the influences of experience and emotions, not even researchers!

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Maybe this time, as we continue to delve deeper into the fog, we may be capable of verifying some of these speculations, or at the very least...  make some progress towards the right direction?

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Heh, who knows... Right from the start, I wasn’t even sure if we could complete the investigation smoothly and reach till the end, thus I my mind was only set on completing the task.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: Now after actually setting foot inside the fog, I’ve grown concerned of all of this... If Hui was there, I’m sure she’d share the same feeling as I am now.

items.speaker: Jiuyue
items.text: There’s only one suppressor left. Here’s hoping the fog would clear out once we activate it, and reveal the “source”... We will find out soon enough.

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: The bird-shaped TD relies on your power to keep resurfacing, and you guys finally catch up with the guy who is obsessed and refuses to wake up.

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: The madness disperses, and Mourning Aix finally seems to recognize the presence that has been chasing after it - the one it tried to resurrect, its former mate.

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: And what's left is fleeting, time just for them.
